base_case: McCarty v. Pheasant Run, Inc., 826 F.2d 1554 (7th Cir. 1987)
constructor: Venkateshwar Reddy Jambula (drafted with Claude Opus as programming and writing assistant)

perturbations.original_text: The burden on the hotel of implementing additional security measures — chain locks on connecting doors, guest warning signs, nighttime security patrols, or keycard-access enhancements — including both direct cost and operational friction.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (burden of precaution) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Auto-generated template rewrite for factor f1 (burden_of_precaution). Human review recommended before final dataset admission.

perturbations.original_text: The probability of an intruder incident occurring at the hotel given the location, prior incident history, and the physical characteristics of the connecting courtyard door.
perturbations.neutral_rewrite: Whether this consideration (probability of harm) supports or opposes the outcome is treated by the court as neither clearly established nor clearly refuted on this record; the factor is present but is not developed as a load-bearing element of the analysis.
perturbations.perturbation_note: Auto-generated template rewrite for factor f2 (probability_of_harm). Human review recommended before final dataset admission.

perturbations.original_text: The gravity of harm if an intruder incident occurs — physical injury, emotional distress, property loss.

perturbations.original_text: The availability of alternative precautions beyond the single chain lock proposed — warning signs in the guest room, a reservation-time notice, a 24-hour front-desk watch, connecting-door disablement.

perturbations.original_text: Plaintiff's own conduct — specifically whether she chained the inside lock on the connecting door, which was available and known to her.
